title: Facets, Calendars, Sessions, Pagination, Search, and Infinite URL Spaces · Contract Workshop
summary: A public-interest site-quality team incrementally builds URL-space trap detector and budget policy from original fixed response and HTML fixtures while retaining earlier authorization, HTTP, URL, provenance, politeness, accessibility, testing, security, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of URL-space trap detector and budget policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Detect repeated filter dimensions, permutations, empty states, sort variants, and low-value combinations using explicit site knowledge.
Bound date ranges, next and previous traversal, generated archives, and cyclic calendar links against stakeholder time scope.
Identify session, tracking, nonce, cache-buster, and personalized parameters without deleting unknown keys by guesswork.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Faceted Navigation Traps
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop site-quality team handles case 2364 during a multilingual public-service site review. Build deterministic Python evidence to detect faceted, calendar, session, pagination, search, and query-space traps before resource budgets fail; all network and parser work must remain below explicit budgets; then introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Faceted Navigation Traps.

For Authorized crawling and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Faceted Navigation Traps, keep Faceted Navigation Traps connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Url Space Budget Defense
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-url-space-budget-defense-df8y21" that uses case 8761 to detect faceted, calendar, session, pagination, search, and query-space traps before resource budgets fail. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Url Space Budget Defense.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Url Space Budget Defense into observable performance evidence. For Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Url Space Budget Defense, keep Url Space Budget Defense connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-url-space-budget-defense-df8y21
# Competency: scraper-url-space-budget-defense.
# Case 8761: a privacy-minimized internal crawl.
# Operating constraint: all network and parser work must remain below explicit budgets.
# Changed case: change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope.
def evidence_scraper_url_space_budget_defense_df8y21(discovered_urls: int = 240, admitted_urls: int = 72, trap_rejections: int = 168, content_identities: int = 60, budget_breaches: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_df8y21 = "df8y21-12y8fd-8761"
    assert evidence_variant_df8y21
    evidence_axes_df8y21 = (
        "authorizationdf8y21",
        "url8761",
        "response12y8fd",
        "treedf8y218761",
        "frontier8761df8y21",
        "report12y8fd8761",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_df8y21)) == 6
    if discovered_urls < 1 or admitted_urls < 0 or trap_rejections < 0:
        return False, discovered_urls, "url-space-count"
    if admitted_urls + trap_rejections != discovered_urls:
        return False, admitted_urls, "trap-reconciliation"
    if content_identities < 1 or content_identities > admitted_urls:
        return False, content_identities, "content-yield"
    if budget_breaches > 0:
        return False, budget_breaches, "budget-breach"
    assert admitted_urls + trap_rejections == discovered_urls
    return True, trap_rejections, "bounded-url-space"
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

options.text: Keep the plausible Url Space Budget Defense output and hide the violated URL-space trap detector and budget policy assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Url Space Budget Defense.

For test an acceptance boundary, crawl one convenient page, omit owner authorization, revision, URL and robots policy, response budgets, parser and selector limits, provenance, frontier and concurrency evidence, changed fixtures, privacy, security, accessibility, stop, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as crawler proof. This does not satisfy Url Space Budget Defense for URL-space trap detector and budget policy.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Url Space Budget Defense condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
